title: Fixing Broken HTML Anchor Tags with Invalid Target Attributes

statement.markdown: Given an array of HTML anchor tags, validate their target attributes and return a boolean array indicating whether each tag has a valid target attribute. A valid target attribute is either `blank`, `_blank`, `_self`, `_parent`, or `_top`.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array of strings, where each string represents an HTML anchor tag.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a boolean array of the same length as the input, where each boolean represents whether the corresponding anchor tag has a valid target attribute.

constraints.description: The number of HTML anchor tags
The maximum number of characters in an anchor tag

examples.explanation: The first anchor tag has a valid target attribute, the second does not, and the third has an invalid target attribute.

examples.input:
<a href='#' target='_self'>
<a href='#' target='_parent'>
<a href='#' target='_top'>
examples.explanation: All three anchor tags have valid target attributes.

hints.text: Split the anchor tag into its parts and extract the target attribute

editorial.title: Validating HTML Anchor Tags
editorial.intuition: To validate the target attribute, we need to check if it matches one of the valid values.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use a loop to iterate over each anchor tag and check its target attribute manually.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a regular expression to extract the target attribute from each anchor tag and then check if it matches one of the valid values.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a regular expression to extract the target attribute from each anchor tag and then check if it matches one of the valid values. We can use a set to store the valid values for efficient lookups.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The regular expression will correctly extract the target attribute from each anchor tag, and the set will correctly identify whether each target attribute is valid.
editorial.dryRun: Let's try to validate the target attribute of the first anchor tag: <a href='#' target='_blank'>
commonMistakes: forgetting to handle invalid target attributes
using a regular expression that is too broad

testCases.input:
<a href='#' target='_self'>
<a href='#' target='_parent'>
<a href='#' target='_top'>
